problem_and_motivation.notes: Explored driver (operator demand + market gap), status quo (fragmented workarounds), and consequences of not building.

stakeholders_and_impact.notes: Identified both sides (staff + customers), business justification (retention + acquisition, core feature), and that it's the first public-facing capability.

feasibility.notes: Explored lane type impact on pricing/duration, revenue model fit with existing entitlements, scale/concurrency needs, and external integration requirements. Codebase exploration answered most feasibility questions silently.

high_level_scope.notes: Confirmed v1 in-scope capabilities and deferred items. User added customer self-cancellation.

key_risks_and_assumptions.notes: Explored Stripe onboarding risk, lane inventory complexity, timezone handling, setup friction. User surfaced guest checkout assumption.

recommendation.notes: Derived from all domains. Go verdict based on strong technical fit, validated demand, and manageable scope.

scope_level_discipline.notes: All questions stayed at capability/concept level. No questions about specific approval flows, field-level decisions, permission rules, workflow steps, or data model details. Lane type question (Turn 5) stayed at 'does it affect pricing/duration' not 'what are the specific pricing rules'. Revenue model question (Turn 6) stayed at 'pay-per-session vs member credits' not 'what discount percentage'. Cancellation (Turn 10-11) stayed at 'customers can cancel within a window' not 'what's the exact window or refund policy'.

define_suggestion_text: Recommend proceeding to `/define` to nail down the functional requirements.
codebase_findings_used_in_questions: bookingRules JSONB on org settings (silent, informed feasibility)
Customer model supports linked and standalone records (Turn 16)
Entitlement system for member session credits (Turn 6)
